key_points: Time measurement helps us understand and compare durations of events
Motion occurs when an object changes its position with respect to time
Distance measures the total path travelled by an object
Speed indicates how quickly an object covers distance in a given time
Uniform motion occurs when an object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time
examples: If a bicycle travels 20 km in 2 hours, its speed is 10 km/h
A train covering equal distances every hour shows uniform motion
If a bus travels 120 km in 3 hours, speed = 40 km/h
Walking from home to school is an example of motion
exam_tips: Identify whether the problem asks for speed, distance or time
Use the correct formula before solving numerical problems
Understand the concept of uniform motion
